ranking.title: Two-Phase Mechanoenhancer Activation Constitutes a Temporal Coincidence Gate
ranking.score_rationale: Novelty: No prior two-phase temporal dependency model for mechanoenhancers (8). Mechanistic specificity: BRD4 heterogeneous nucleation on H3K27ac independently confirmed by 2024 literature; Piezo1→CaMKII→HDAC4 well-characterized (8). Testability: dCas9-p300 rescue experiment is a clean discriminating test; CUT&RUN time course feasible (9). Groundedness: 75% claims verified; INFERRED claim supported by independent BRD4 condensation physics (7). Cross-domain: bridges condensation physics, calcium signaling, and mechanoenhancer epigenomics (7). Impact: if confirmed, redefines mechanoenhancer activation from parallel to sequential model (8).

ranking.title: Lamin A/C Concentration Sets the Cell-Intrinsic Stiffness-Sensing Threshold for Mechanoenhancer Activation
ranking.score_rationale: Novelty: Core concept (lamin A/C → YAP threshold) published in 2024 Nat Comms PMID 39578439; only mechanoenhancer H3K27ac extension is new (4). Mechanistic specificity: all components well-characterized but mechanism is a transitive chain with YAP as bottleneck; multiple other YAP regulators not considered (6). Testability: scCUT&RUN + lamin A IF + EC50 shift experiments well-designed (8). Groundedness: 80% claims verified; all citations confirmed (8). Cross-domain: stays within mechanobiology — lamin mechanics to enhancers is incremental (4). Impact: would confirm expected behavior rather than reveal new principle (5).

eliminated.title: Nuclear Actin Polymerization State Regulates MRTF-A Residence Time at CaRG-Box Mechanoenhancers
eliminated.reason: KILLED by Critic — fabricated protein property (RPEL-F-actin binding structurally impossible)

diversity_check.note: C2-H1 (temporal dependency / condensate physics) and C2-H2 (nuclear mechanics / cell heterogeneity) are mechanistically distinct

elo_tournament.note: Skipped — only 2 hypotheses, pairwise comparison trivial. C2-H1 dominates C2-H2 on novelty, mechanism, and impact.

matches.reason: Superior novelty (8 vs 4), stronger mechanism confirmation, higher impact
